label: C
description: General-purpose programming language used in systems and low-level software.

label: C++
description: Object-oriented extension of C, widely used in systems, video games and high-performance software.

label: C#
description: Language in the .NET ecosystem for desktop, web and cloud applications.

label: Go
description: Efficient language for distributed systems and high-performance services.

label: Java
description: Complete ecosystem for enterprise and mobile applications.

label: JavaScript
description: Key language for web development and interactive applications.

label: TypeScript
description: Typed superset of JavaScript for scalable projects.

label: Python
description: Versatile language used in data science, web, automation and more.

label: PHP
description: Popular language for backend and content management systems.

label: Ruby
description: Agile and expressive language used in web development.

label: R
description: Language for statistics and advanced data analysis.

label: Rust
description: Modern language focused on safety and memory efficiency.

label: Kotlin
description: Modern language for Android and the JVM.

label: Swift
description: Official language for developing on iOS and macOS.

label: Objective-C
description: Traditional language for the Apple ecosystem, predecessor to Swift.

label: Scala
description: Functional and object-oriented language for the JVM, used in big data and distributed systems.

label: Dart
description: Language used with Flutter for cross-platform apps.

label: Haskell
description: Pure functional language with an advanced type system.

label: Erlang
description: Language for distributed and fault-tolerant systems.

label: Elixir
description: Functional language based on the Erlang virtual machine, focused on concurrency and fault tolerance.

label: Smalltalk
description: Object-oriented language pioneering modern programming, known for its interactive environment and focus on simplicity.

label: Groovy
description: JVM language with flexible and dynamic syntax, used in automation, scripting and frameworks like Grails.

label: VB.NET
description: Programming language in the .NET ecosystem used for desktop, web and automation applications in the Microsoft environment.

label: Angular
description: Framework for building scalable web applications, developed by Google.

label: React
description: Component-based user interface library, developed by Meta.

label: Vue.js
description: Progressive framework for web interfaces, lightweight and easy to adopt.

label: Ionic
description: Framework for mobile apps using web technologies.

label: Flutter
description: Google's framework for creating cross-platform apps with Dart.

label: Django
description: Robust backend framework for web applications in Python.

label: Node.js
description: Environment for running JavaScript on the server, based on the V8 engine.

label: Ruby on Rails
description: Web framework that favors productivity and convention over configuration.

label: Symfony
description: PHP framework for robust and scalable backend development.

label: ASP.NET
description: Microsoft's framework for web applications in .NET.

label: Xamarin
description: Microsoft's framework for cross-platform mobile apps with C#.

label: Akka
description: Toolkit and runtime for building distributed systems and highly concurrent applications based on the actor model.

label: Sinatra
description: Minimalist Ruby framework for creating web services and APIs in a lightweight and fast way.

label: Svelte
description: JavaScript framework that compiles components into efficient imperative code, without virtual DOM.

label: Next.js
description: React framework for web applications with server-side rendering (SSR), static generation (SSG) and App Router.

label: Nuxt
description: Vue.js framework for web applications with SSR, SSG and a full-stack development experience.

label: Astro
description: Content-oriented web framework that generates static HTML by default and allows using components from any framework.

label: HTMX
description: Library that allows accessing AJAX, WebSockets and Server-Sent Events directly from HTML without writing JavaScript.

label: JavaScript Frameworks
description: Ecosystem of JavaScript frameworks and libraries for developing modern web applications, both on the client and server side.

label: Software Architecture
description: High-level design of systems and their components, including structural and quality decisions.

label: Event-Driven Architecture
description: Event-based architecture for decoupled and reactive systems.

label: Domain-Driven Design
description: Design approach that models software around the domain and business logic.

label: Microservices
description: Distributed architecture based on independent, separately deployable services.

label: Behavior-Driven Development
description: Development methodology that defines system behavior using business-understandable examples.

label: Test-Driven Development
description: Practice of writing tests before production code to guide design and prevent regressions.

label: Software Craftsmanship
description: Movement that promotes quality, good practices and professional responsibility in software development.

label: Functional Programming
description: Paradigm based on pure functions, immutability and composition.

label: Object-Oriented Programming
description: Paradigm based on classes, objects, inheritance and encapsulation.

label: Clean Code
description: Best practices for writing readable, maintainable code free of technical debt.

label: Design Patterns
description: Reusable solutions to common software design problems.

label: Distributed Systems
description: Techniques and architectures for building systems that run across multiple coordinated nodes, with emphasis on scalability and fault tolerance.

label: Low-code / No-code
description: Application development paradigm that minimizes or eliminates traditional code writing through visual interfaces, configurable workflows and pre-built components.

label: AWS
description: Amazon Web Services. Amazon's cloud services platform.

label: Microsoft Azure
description: Microsoft's cloud platform for computing, storage and managed services.

label: Google Cloud
description: Google's cloud infrastructure and services.

label: Docker
description: Container platform for packaging and deploying applications in a reproducible way.

label: Kubernetes
description: Container orchestrator for scalable and fault-tolerant deployments.

label: Terraform
description: Infrastructure-as-code tool for managing cloud resources declaratively.

label: CI/CD
description: Automation of software build, testing and continuous deployment.

label: Jenkins
description: Open-source continuous integration tool widely used in CI/CD pipelines.

label: Virtualization
description: Virtual machine technology for running multiple systems on the same hardware.

label: Networking & Connectivity
description: Networks, protocols and connectivity between systems.

label: Systems Administration
description: Management and maintenance of IT systems, servers and production environments.

label: Bash / Shell Scripting
description: Scripting language for Unix systems that allows automating tasks and managing the system via the command line.

label: Servers
description: Administration, configuration and maintenance of physical or virtual servers for deploying and operating applications and services.

label: Git
description: Distributed version control system for managing history and collaboration in software projects.

label: Visual Studio Code
description: Lightweight and extensible code editor from Microsoft, widely used for web development, Python and other languages.

label: n8n
description: Open-source workflow automation platform focused on API integration and no-code processes.

label: AI Coding Agents
description: Artificial intelligence tools for assisting in writing, reviewing and generating code, such as Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor or Codex.

label: GitHub
description: Repository hosting platform and collaboration in software projects, with features for code review, issues, discussions and GitHub Actions.

label: SQL Databases
description: Relational databases based on the tabular model and SQL language, such as PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle or SQL Server.

label: NoSQL Databases
description: Non-relational databases designed for high scalability, flexibility or distributed data, such as MongoDB, Cassandra, Redis or DynamoDB.

label: Graph Databases
description: Technologies oriented to modeling complex relationships between entities using nodes and edges, such as Neo4j or JanusGraph.

label: Distributed Storage
description: Systems for storing data replicated or partitioned across multiple nodes, offering resilience and horizontal scalability.

label: Data Warehouse
description: Platforms optimized for business analytics, complex queries and historical models of large data volumes.

label: Data Lake
description: Massive repositories that store data in its original format for subsequent analysis and machine learning.

label: ETL & Data Pipelines
description: Processes and tools for moving, transforming and integrating data between systems in an automated way.

label: DataOps
description: Practices for ensuring quality, governance and automation in the data lifecycle, applying DevOps principles to the data world.

label: Spatial Databases
description: Database systems specialized in storing, indexing and querying geospatial and cartographic data.

label: Data Science
description: Extraction of knowledge and value from data using statistics, machine learning and visualization.

label: Statistics
description: Quantitative analysis and modeling to understand and predict phenomena from data.

label: Data Engineering
description: Building and maintaining data pipelines, platforms and architectures.

label: Big Data
description: Management and processing of large data volumes with tools like Hadoop or Apache Spark.

label: Data Visualization
description: Visual representation of information to communicate insights clearly and effectively.

label: Machine Learning
description: Algorithms and models that learn patterns from data to make predictions or decisions.

label: Deep Learning
description: Deep learning models based on neural networks for vision, language and more.

label: Natural Language Processing
description: Techniques for computers to understand, generate and analyze text or speech in human language.

label: Business Intelligence
description: Strategies, tools and practices for transforming data into actionable information for decision-making.

label: Geospatial / GIS
description: Location intelligence, geolocation, maps, spatial analysis and geographic information systems.

label: MLOps
description: Set of practices for automating and managing the machine learning model lifecycle in production, combining ML with DevOps principles.

label: Generative AI
description: AI field focused on models capable of generating text, images, code or other content, such as GPT, Stable Diffusion or DALL-E.

label: Large Language Models
description: Large-scale language models trained on large text corpora to understand and generate natural language.

label: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description: Technique that combines information retrieval with language models to generate responses grounded in relevant data.

label: Vector Databases
description: Storage systems specialized in embeddings and semantic similarity search, used in AI and RAG applications.

label: Web Analytics
description: Techniques and tools for measuring, analyzing and interpreting user behavior on websites and applications, with the goal of optimizing experience and performance.

label: Cybersecurity
description: Protection of systems, networks and data against attacks, unauthorized access and vulnerabilities.

label: Ethical Hacking
description: Authorized penetration testing to detect vulnerabilities before malicious actors do.

label: Pentesting
description: Security assessment of systems through controlled attacks and hacking techniques.

label: Cloud Security
description: Protection of cloud environments, data and services against threats and insecure configurations.

label: Web Security
description: Practices and techniques to protect web applications against common attacks such as XSS, SQLi or CSRF.

label: Cryptography
description: Mathematical techniques to protect information through encryption, digital signatures and secure protocols.

label: Digital Forensics
description: Forensic analysis of systems and devices to investigate security incidents.

label: Arduino
description: Open-source hardware and software platform for electronic prototyping.

label: Raspberry Pi
description: Low-cost microcomputer widely used in educational, maker and IoT projects.

label: Internet of Things
description: Connected devices and systems that collect, send and process data from the physical environment.

label: Sensors
description: Smart sensors for measurements of the physical environment, used in IoT, industry and maker projects.

label: Robotics
description: Design, construction and programming of robots and autonomous systems.

label: 3D Printing
description: Additive manufacturing to create physical prototypes and parts from digital models.

label: Makerspace
description: Shared creation, experimentation and manufacturing spaces where the maker community gathers.

label: Android
description: Google's mobile platform for developing applications on smartphones and tablets.

label: iOS
description: Apple's operating system for iPhone and iPad, with development in Swift or Objective-C.

label: Linux
description: Open-source operating system widely used in servers, cloud and development.

label: Windows
description: Microsoft's operating system widely used in personal and enterprise environments.

label: macOS
description: Apple's operating system for Mac computers, Unix-based and oriented to productivity and development.

label: Virtual Reality
description: Immersive experiences through VR devices that simulate digital environments.

label: Augmented Reality
description: Overlay of digital information on the physical environment in real time.

label: Mixed Reality
description: Technologies that combine virtual and augmented reality to create immersive experiences that integrate physical and digital objects.

label: 3D Modeling
description: Creation of three-dimensional models for video games, visualization, architecture or manufacturing.

label: Visual Effects (VFX)
description: Digital visual effects for film, video games and audiovisual production.

label: Game Development
description: Design, programming and production of video games for any platform.

label: Game Design
description: Design of mechanics, levels, narrative and gameplay in video games.

label: Indie Games
description: Independent video game development without backing from major publishers.

label: Unity
description: Engine for developing games and interactive experiences in 2D and 3D.

label: Unreal Engine
description: Advanced video game engine used to create 3D experiences, video games, simulations and high-quality interactive content.

label: WordPress
description: World's most used content management system, ideal for blogs, corporate websites and stores.

label: WooCommerce
description: E-commerce plugin for WordPress, widely used in online stores.

label: PrestaShop
description: Open-source platform to create online stores, very popular in Europe.

label: Drupal
description: Flexible and modular CMS for web portals and applications with complex requirements.

label: Liferay
description: Enterprise platform for digital portals, content management and corporate intranets.

label: Joomla!
description: Open-source content management system (CMS), widely used to build corporate websites, portals and web applications.

label: Tech for Good
description: Technology initiatives focused on positive social impact and the common good.

label: Digital Activism
description: Use of technologies to promote social causes and defend online rights.

label: Tech Ethics
description: Reflection and practice of moral principles, responsibility and good practices in technology.

label: Diversity & Inclusion
description: Initiatives that foster participation of people of different genders, races and backgrounds in technology.

label: Women in Tech
description: Movements and groups that support the participation and visibility of women in technology.

label: Health Tech
description: Applications and technologies applied to the medical, health and wellness field.

label: STEM
description: Areas of science, technology, engineering and mathematics focused on education, outreach and vocations.

label: Mentoring
description: Initiatives focused on connecting mentors and learners for professional development in technology.

label: Free Software
description: Groups that promote software freedom, open licenses and community development.

label: Open Data
description: Use, publication and analysis of open data for social, institutional or transparency projects.

label: Open Hardware
description: Movement that promotes open hardware design, allowing anyone to study, modify and manufacture devices freely.

label: Open Source
description: Software developed with open code, available to be used, modified and distributed by anyone.

label: Startups
description: Entrepreneurial communities focused on launching and growing new technology companies.

label: Product Management
description: Strategic management of digital products, from vision to roadmap evolution.

label: Growth
description: Strategies and experiments to drive user growth, retention and revenue.

label: Lean Startup
description: Methodology to create and validate products efficiently, based on rapid learning cycles.

label: Innovation
description: Exploration and application of new ideas, technologies and business models to generate value.

label: SEO
description: Search engine optimization to improve visibility and organic traffic.

label: Digital Marketing
description: Online promotion strategies, including social media, content, email and digital advertising.

label: Gamification
description: Application of game dynamics, mechanics and design elements in non-game contexts to motivate behaviors, improve learning or increase engagement.

label: Blockchain
description: Distributed and immutable ledger technology for transactions and contracts without intermediaries.

label: Cryptocurrencies
description: Decentralized digital assets based on cryptography, such as Bitcoin or Ethereum.

label: Ethereum
description: Decentralized platform for smart contracts and distributed applications (dApps).

label: Web3
description: Ecosystem of decentralized technologies based on blockchain that allows creating applications, identities and services without intermediaries.

label: HTML
description: Standard markup language for structuring content in web pages and applications.

label: CSS
description: Style language that controls the visual presentation of HTML documents: colors, typography, layouts and animations.

label: Web Accessibility
description: Design and development of websites and applications usable by people with functional diversity, following WCAG guidelines.

label: Web Performance
description: Optimization of load speed, runtime performance and real user metrics (Core Web Vitals).

label: Web Standards
description: W3C, WHATWG and TC39 specifications that define how browsers and the web platform should work.

label: Frontend Tooling
description: Frontend ecosystem tools: bundlers, transpilers, linters, formatters and development environments.

label: Browser APIs
description: Native browser APIs: Web Workers, Service Workers, WebSockets, WebRTC, Fetch, Storage and others.

label: Design Systems
description: Collections of reusable components, style guides and design principles that unify digital products.

label: UX/UI
description: Design disciplines focused on user experience and the visual interface of digital products.
